subject: everyday-science
description: Scientific Instruments & Measurements MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
mcqs.explanation: A barometer is used to measure atmospheric pressure — and assists in forecasting weather and determining altitude.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure wind speed?
mcqs.explanation: An anemometer measures wind speed (and sometimes direction).

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure humidity of air?
mcqs.explanation: A hygrometer is used to measure the humidity of air or a gas.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure temperature?
mcqs.explanation: A thermometer is the basic instrument used to measure temperature.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure altitude (especially in aircraft)?
mcqs.explanation: An altimeter is used to determine altitude attained — especially the barometric or radar device fitted in an aircraft.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure rainfall?
mcqs.explanation: A udometer (also called hyetometer) is used for measuring rainfall.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure blood pressure?
mcqs.explanation: A sphygmomanometer is used to measure blood pressure — especially arterial blood pressure.

mcqs.q: A stethoscope is used to:
options: Detect sounds produced in the body (heart, lungs etc.)
mcqs.explanation: A stethoscope is a medical instrument for detecting sounds produced in the body, conveyed to the ears via rubber tubing.

mcqs.q: Which instrument records the electrical changes occurring during the heartbeat?
mcqs.explanation: An electrocardiograph (ECG) records the changes of electrical potential occurring during the heartbeat — used to diagnose heart abnormalities.

mcqs.q: Which instrument records brain waves?
mcqs.explanation: An electroencephalograph (EEG) is the apparatus for detecting and recording brain waves.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure the acuity of hearing?
mcqs.explanation: An audiometer is used in measuring the acuity of hearing.

mcqs.q: An endoscope is used to:
options: Visualise the interior of a hollow organ (such as the bladder or oesophagus)
mcqs.explanation: An endoscope is an illuminated tubular instrument used for visualising the interior of a hollow organ for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to examine the stomach?
mcqs.explanation: A gastroscope is an optical instrument used for inspecting the interior of the stomach.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to examine the bladder?
mcqs.explanation: A cystoscope is used for examining the bladder — passing through the urethra.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to inspect the retina and other parts of the eye?
mcqs.explanation: An ophthalmoscope is an instrument for inspecting the retina and other parts of the eye.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to examine the ear (tympanic membrane)?
mcqs.explanation: An otoscope (with lighting and magnifying systems) is used for visual examination of the tympanic membrane and ear canal.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to examine the larynx?
mcqs.explanation: A laryngoscope is an endoscope for examining the interior of the larynx.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to detect and record earthquakes?
mcqs.explanation: A seismograph (or seismometer) is the instrument that measures and records details of earthquakes — such as force and duration.

mcqs.q: Which scale is used to measure the magnitude of earthquakes?
mcqs.explanation: The Richter scale is the measure of the strength (magnitude) of earthquakes.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures gas or vapour pressure?
mcqs.explanation: A manometer is an instrument for measuring the pressure of gases and vapours.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures very high temperatures (beyond a mercury thermometer's range)?
mcqs.explanation: A pyrometer is used for measuring temperatures especially when beyond the range of mercurial thermometers.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures heat (quantities of absorbed or emitted)?
mcqs.explanation: A calorimeter is an apparatus for measuring quantities of absorbed or emitted heat or for determining specific heats.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to detect the presence of a metallic object or measure magnetic-field intensity?
mcqs.explanation: A magnetometer is used to detect the presence of a metallic object or measure the intensity of a magnetic field.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure underwater depth using sound?
mcqs.explanation: A fathometer is used for measuring underwater depth using sound (sonar).

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures the precise angle (e.g. in surveying)?
mcqs.explanation: A goniometer is an instrument for the precise measurement of angles.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure the density of liquids?
mcqs.explanation: A hydrometer is an instrument for measuring the density of liquids.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure the density of milk?
mcqs.explanation: A lactometer is an instrument for measuring the density of milk.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures the amount of sugar in a solution?
mcqs.explanation: A saccharimeter is a device for measuring the amount of sugar in a solution.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures the amount of salt in a solution?
mcqs.explanation: A salinometer (such as a hydrometer) is used to measure the amount of salt in a solution.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures the refractive index of a substance?
mcqs.explanation: A refractometer is used to measure refractive indices — useful in identification or sugar-content determination.

mcqs.q: Which instrument detects or measures small electric currents?
mcqs.explanation: A galvanometer detects or measures a small electric current via the deflection of a magnetic needle/coil.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures electric current in amperes?
mcqs.explanation: An ammeter is the measuring device used to measure electric current in amperes.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures voltage (potential difference)?
mcqs.explanation: A voltmeter is an instrument for measuring (in volts) the difference in potential between two points of an electrical circuit.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures electrical resistance?
mcqs.explanation: An ohmmeter measures resistance directly in ohms.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures electric power?
mcqs.explanation: A wattmeter is the instrument for measuring electric power in watts.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure mechanical force?
mcqs.explanation: A dynamometer is an instrument for measuring mechanical force.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to measure spinal curvature?
mcqs.explanation: A rachiometer is an instrument for measuring spinal curvatures.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to view minute objects with magnification?
mcqs.explanation: A microscope is an optical instrument with a lens or combination of lenses for making enlarged images of minute objects.

mcqs.q: Which instrument is used to view distant objects?
mcqs.explanation: A telescope is a usually tubular optical instrument for viewing distant objects.

mcqs.q: Which instrument measures the speed of a vehicle?
mcqs.explanation: A speedometer indicates the speed of a vehicle — usually combined with an odometer that records the distance travelled.

mcqs.q: Which instrument records the distance travelled by a vehicle?
mcqs.explanation: An odometer is the instrument for measuring the distance travelled by a wheeled vehicle.

mcqs.q: Which instrument records distance covered by a person on foot?
mcqs.explanation: A pedometer is an instrument (usually in watch form) that records the distance a person covers on foot by responding to body motion at each step.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of length?
mcqs.explanation: The metre (m) is the SI base unit of length.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of mass?
mcqs.explanation: The kilogram (kg) is the SI base unit of mass.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of time?
mcqs.explanation: The second (s) is the SI base unit of time.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of electric current?
mcqs.explanation: The ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of thermodynamic temperature?
mcqs.explanation: The kelvin (K) is the SI base unit of thermodynamic temperature.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of amount of substance?
mcqs.explanation: The mole (mol) is the SI base unit of amount of substance.

mcqs.q: What is the SI base unit of luminous intensity?
mcqs.explanation: The candela (cd) is the SI base unit of luminous intensity.

mcqs.q: What is the SI unit of force?
mcqs.explanation: The newton (N) is the SI unit of force.

mcqs.q: What is the SI unit of pressure (stress)?
mcqs.explanation: The pascal (Pa) is the SI unit of pressure (stress).

mcqs.q: What is the SI unit of work / energy / heat?
mcqs.explanation: The joule (J) is the SI unit of work, energy and heat.

mcqs.q: What is the SI unit of frequency?
mcqs.explanation: The hertz (Hz) is the SI unit of frequency (one cycle per second).

mcqs.q: Which unit is used to measure the speed of a ship?
mcqs.explanation: The knot is used to measure the speed of a ship (1 knot ≈ 1.852 km/h).

mcqs.q: A 'light year' is a unit of measurement for:
mcqs.explanation: A light year is a unit of distance used in astronomy — the distance light travels in one year.

mcqs.q: What is a 'parsec' used to measure?
mcqs.explanation: A parsec (Pc) is used to measure distance in astronomy (about 3.26 light years).

mcqs.q: The unit 'carat' is used to measure:
options: Mass of precious stones / purity of gold
mcqs.explanation: The carat is used to measure the mass of precious stones and the purity of gold.
